title: Tipping Culture in China
metaDescription: Learn about tipping etiquette in China. Know where and when to tip for a smooth travel experience.

sections.heading: h1
sections.content: Tipping is not traditionally expected in China, especially in local establishments like small restaurants, taxis, or for basic services. It is generally not a part of everyday transactions.

sections.heading: h2
sections.content: In the tourism industry, a small tip may be appreciated for exceptional service, such as from a tour guide or hotel porter. Discretion is key; presenting a tip discreetly in an envelope is often considered polite.

sections.heading: h3
sections.content: Practical tip: Don't feel obligated. If you receive outstanding service and wish to tip, rounding up a bill or offering 10-20 CNY is usually sufficient. A genuine 'thank you' is always valued.

keyTakeaways: Tipping is not a standard expectation in most everyday situations.
It may be appreciated for exceptional service in tourism contexts.
When in doubt, a polite 'thank you' is a safe and friendly gesture.
